title: Add --effort CLI param with xhigh level for Rick's heavy-thinking commands
source_context: Opus 4.7 adaptive thinking is off by default and effort controls depth. Our existing thinking_budget logic at delegate.py:1160-1166 maps complexity to thinking_budget integer; replace with effort string. XL/L=xhigh, M=high, S=medium. [PRIORITY: Opus 4.7 migration wave — user-requested]
description: Opus 4.7 exposes an effort parameter with levels {low, medium, high, xhigh, max} — Anthropic recommends xhigh as the default for coding/agentic use cases. Rick's plan, scan, and delegate heavy-complexity tickets should use xhigh. Currently we have thinking_budget (being deprecated) and no effort control. Expose --effort argparse flag on: orchestrate.py plan, prometheus.py scan, delegate.py (auto-scale from ticket complexity). When set, pass through to claude CLI via --effort if supported, else inject as prompt directive.
proposed_changes: Add --effort arg with choices [low, medium, high, xhigh, max]. Replace thinking_budget-to-int mapping with complexity-to-effort mapping. Pass to claude CLI via --effort flag (if --help shows support) or otherwise inject into system prompt as: '## EFFORT LEVEL: {level} — reason accordingly.'
